title: Mille-feuille
subtitle: Layered puff pastry with pastry cream and fondant icing
excerpt: Mille-feuille is three layers of baked puff pastry sandwiched with pastry cream and finished with a white fondant icing feathered with chocolate lines. The name means 'a thousand leaves', and a good one has many fine, crisp layers that shatter when a fork goes through. The technique is assembly: bake the pastry, make the cream, layer and ice.
sourceNotes: Mille-feuille is a classic French patisserie preparation. Documented from at least the seventeenth century in French culinary literature. The standard form of three layers of puff pastry with crème pâtissière is firmly public domain. The chocolate-feather icing pattern on the fondant top is the traditional presentation.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the pastry and make the cream up to a day ahead. Assemble within 2 hours of serving, as the pastry softens quickly once filled.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; weight the pastry under a tray to keep it flat and even

content.text: Mille-feuille is three layers of puff pastry with pastry cream between each layer and fondant icing on top. The critical elements are flat, evenly baked pastry; pastry cream set firm enough to pipe; and icing applied quickly before it sets. The feathered chocolate pattern on the fondant is done with a skewer in under a minute; it looks more difficult than it is.

items.prepNote: all-butter, shop-bought or homemade rough puff

content.text: Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the pastry to a thin rectangle, about 3 mm thick. Prick all over with a fork. Place on a lined baking tray, lay a sheet of baking paper on top, and set a second heavy baking tray on top to weigh it flat. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until golden and crisp. The weight prevents the pastry puffing unevenly. Cool completely, then trim to three even rectangles of equal size.

Heat the milk with the vanilla until steaming. Whisk the yolks, sugar, and cornflour in a bowl until smooth. Pour a quarter of the hot milk over the yolk mixture, whisking, then return to the pan. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until the cream thickens and bubbles. Boil for 1 full minute. Pour into a bowl, press cling film directly onto the surface, and refrigerate until cold and firm.
Beat the cold pastry cream to restore smoothness. Pipe or spread generously over the first layer of pastry. Add the second pastry layer and repeat. Top with the third pastry layer.

Mix the fondant icing sugar with enough water to make a spreadable, pourable icing. Pour over the top pastry layer and spread quickly to the edges. Before it sets, pipe parallel lines of melted chocolate across the fondant at 2 cm intervals. Immediately draw a skewer across the chocolate lines in alternating directions, perpendicular to them, to create the feather pattern. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before cutting. Use a sharp, thin-bladed knife and press straight down rather than sawing.
